corrections.text: The article attributes the following quote to Dr. Cindy Jacobs, CMO of Achieve Life Sciences: "Cytisinicline is very selective in targeting only nicotine receptors with limited off-target binding." The source (Achieve Life Sciences IR press release, April 21, 2025) contains a longer statement: "Cytisinicline is very selective in targeting only nicotine receptors and has shown limited binding to other off-target receptors that can cause side effects, like nausea and gastrointestinal disturbances. We believe this leads to a highly tolerable treatment with cytisinicline, as demonstrated in our clinical trial program." The article's version is a paraphrase presented as a verbatim quotation; the original wording is more specific about the clinical significance of selectivity.

The article attributes this quote to Dr. Nancy Rigotti: "Cytisinicline could help many smokers quit and reduce smoking-related health risks." The source (same Achieve IR press release) contains: "The study findings published today suggest that cytisinicline, if approved by the FDA, could help many smokers to quit and reduce the smoking-related risks to their health." The article omits the conditional framing ("The study findings published today suggest that") and slightly paraphrases the wording. The substance of the quote is preserved but the presentation as verbatim is inaccurate.
